Document:
# Blueprints & Crafting
[textlink="View Full Wiki Page" link="https://ss14.misfitsystems.net/wiki/index.php/Crafting"]
Blueprint crafting is the workbench-based side of the Wasteland crafting system. If you have the right blueprint, bench, resources, and tools, you can turn scrap and rare parts into faction gear, armor, and weapons.
## Crafting Basics
[bold]Blueprints[/bold] define what you can make and what each recipe needs.
[bold]T-45 Power Armor Blueprint[/bold] — example BoS blueprint
[bold]Tribal Rifle Blueprint[/bold] — example Tribal blueprint
[bold]Workbenches[/bold] determine where a recipe can be completed.
[bold]Weapon Bench[/bold] — used for weapons and weapon modifications
[bold]Resources[/bold] can be raw materials, scrap parts, or intermediate crafted components.
[bold]Scrap Electronic[/bold] — salvaged from junk
[bold]Advanced Gun Frame[/bold] — intermediate crafting component
[bold]Tools[/bold] are required by many recipes, but they are not consumed during crafting.
[bold]Screwdriver[/bold], [bold]Welder[/bold], [bold]Wrench[/bold]
Some blueprints are faction-locked, so access can depend on who you are and which bench you can use.
## How to Craft
Find or buy a blueprint, gather the listed resources, then take everything to the correct workbench. Open the crafting interface, select the blueprint, and confirm the recipe.
If the recipe is available and you meet the requirements, the crafted item will appear in your inventory or next to the bench.
## Blueprint Categories
Blueprints are found by exploring the Wasteland, looting enemies, or trading with faction vendors.
## Brotherhood of Steel
Power armor restoration and Brotherhood field gear, usually recovered from patrols, bunkers, and chapter stockpiles.
[bold]T-45 Power Armor[/bold] — standard Brotherhood power armor
[bold]T-60 Tesla Armor[/bold] — advanced Brotherhood energy armor
[bold]BoS Initiate Armor[/bold] — entry-level Brotherhood plating
[bold]Midwest Armor[/bold] — Midwest chapter armor pattern
[bold]Midwest Armor MK2[/bold] — upgraded Midwest armor
## NCR
NCR armor patterns and restored military equipment, most commonly found in camps, depots, and quartermaster caches.
[bold]T-45 NCR Armor[/bold] — NCR-modified power armor
[bold]T-51 NCR Armor[/bold] — NCR-modified power armor
[bold]T-60 NCR Armor[/bold] — NCR-modified power armor
[bold]Raider Power Armor[/bold] — salvaged power armor
[bold]Advanced NCR Armor[/bold] — top-tier NCR power armor
[bold]NCR Desert Vest[/bold] — standard NCR field vest
[bold]NCR Combat Armor[/bold] — NCR standard combat armor
[bold]NCR Combat Armor MK2[/bold] — upgraded NCR combat armor
## Legion
Legion armor blueprints recovered from outposts, patrol leaders, and veteran warriors.
[bold]Legionnaire Armor[/bold] — standard Legion armor
[bold]Veteran Legionnaire Armor[/bold] — upgraded Legion armor for veterans
## Tribal
Handcrafted tribal weapons and armor, usually traded by tribal groups or found in remote camps.
[bold]Handcannon[/bold] — .45-70 tribal revolver
[bold]Tribal Handgun[/bold] — .44 tribal revolver
[bold]Tribal Rifle[/bold] — .556 tribal sniper rifle
[bold]Tribal Assault Rifle[/bold] — upgraded .556 tribal rifle
[bold]Tribal Combat Armor[/bold] — handcrafted tribal plating
## Miscellaneous
Faction-neutral blueprints available to any capable crafter with the right bench and parts.
[bold]Laser Revolver[/bold] — energy sidearm
[bold]Makeshift Laser Rifle[/bold] — cobbled-together energy weapon
[bold]Pipe Rifle (.556)[/bold] — improvised ballistic rifle
[bold]Combat Armor[/bold] — standard wasteland combat armor
[bold]Combat Armor MK2[/bold] — upgraded combat armor
